q: The Doppler effect or Doppler shift is the change in ___ of a wave related to an observer.
It is due to the ___ motion between the source and observer.
It is named after the Austrian physicist ___ Doppler.
A common example of Doppler shift is the change of frequency (___) heard when a vehicle sounding a horn approach and recedes from an observer.
The change of frequency heard when a vehicle sounding a horn approaches and recedes from an observer is a common example of ___.
The general Doppler equation is $f_o = f_s \left( \frac{v \pm v_o}{v \mp v_s} \right)$, where $f_o$ is the frequency heard by the ___.
In the Doppler equation, $f_s$ represents the frequency produced by the ___.
When the observer moves away from the source, the sign used for $v_o$ is ___.
When the source moves away from the observer, the sign used for $v_s$ is ___.
The wavelength of light from a star shifts towards the red end of the spectrum when the earth and the star are moving ___ from each other.
Radar stands for radio detection and ___.
Doppler ultrasound is a technique that can reveal blockage to blood flow and show the formation of ___ in arteries.
